sections.heading: Sector 1
sections.body:
To start your lap, you will be flying into turn one and brake-neck speed from the main straight. Brake just after the 100m board, and drop to 2nd gear for Turn 1, a tight left-hander at the top of a steep hill. Be smooth on the brakes — locking up here will send you wide and into the run off, or worse, the barrier.

Immediately flick the car right into Turn 2. Use all the track and mount the inside curb slightly, but dont run too wide on exit, or you can say goodbye to the car behind as they fly past you on the following straight with DRS.

Following the straight is a flat-out left corner. Keep it smooth and open up the steering early. Stay to the right to maximise your line and prepare for the long DRS straight. This section is critical — a poor exit from Turn 2 can cost a significant amount of time here, especially leading up to the long and complex Sector 2.

sections.heading: Sector 2
sections.body:
Brake just after the 100m board, heading downhill. Drop to 3rd gear for this tight left-hander. Trail brake slightly and hug the inside curb. Carry momentum and get back onto the power as early as you dare to. Its a solid overtaking spot when cars are in race trim, just be mindful of how quickly you can ruin your momentum in this section with overshooting your braking zone.

A short uphill right kink taken flat or with a slight lift, depending on your setup and tyre wear. Stay tight to the right to position yourself correctly for the following sequence.

Turn 6 is a long right-hander. Brake lightly and carry speed through in 4th gear, using a wide entry to swing back in toward the apex at the bottom of the corner.

Turn 7 is a quick kink — stay smooth and dont lift too much. Its all about maintaining rhythm and minimising steering input. A tight left with a quick braking zone comes next. Drop to 2nd gear and ensure you trail brake to rotate the car, then get an early start on the power. Avoid aggressive kerb use — it can unsettle the car in F1 25.

A short run into a tight right-hand hairpin. Stay in 2nd gear, brake early and focus on a clean exit. Throttle modulation is key — the rear tyres can easily lose grip here, especially late in the race.

This sharp left-hand hairpin is one of the slowest on the track. Brake hard, down to 1st or 2nd gear, and rotate the car quickly. Short shift on exit to reduce any wheelspin you have on this slightly off-camber section.

Following the hairpin is a downhill, sweeping right. Easy to overshoot. Brake lightly, stay in 3rd or 4th gear, and keep the car balanced. A good exit here is crucial, as it leads to the final corners and the uphill blast.

sections.heading: Sector 3
sections.body:
A crucial left-hand corner leading onto the final straight. Brake at around 50m, drop to 3rd gear, and hug the inside. The exit here is everything. Get the power down early and cleanly to maximise speed up the hill.

This is a long, sweeping climb through flat-out kinks that lead onto the start/finish straight. You will be able to open the DRS and keep it pinned all the way until the braking zone of turn one. ERS deployment is highly effective here, especially in qualifying and final-lap duels to the line on low fuel.
